intro: Microstylis rheedei, also known as the Kerala orchid, is a type of orchid species native to the Western Ghats of India. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in regulating blood sugar levels and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Microstylis rheedei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, making it a common herbal preparation in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
